2606.22082.title: CodeTeam: An LLM-Powered Multi-Agent Framework for Repository-Level Code Generation
2606.22082.paper_summary: Natural language to repository generation (NL2Repo) requires a system to construct an entire software repository from a natural-language requirements document. Compared with function-level code generation, this task demands longer planning horizons, stable interfaces across files, and iterative debugging of cross-file inconsistencies. To address these challenges, we propose CodeTeam, an LLM-based multi-agent framework that separates planning, decision making, and implementation into distinct, coordinated stages. In the planning stage, multiple Architect agents draft competing software design sketches (SDS), optionally grounded by retrieved design references. A CTO agent then evaluates, selects, and normalizes the most promising SDS into a machine-checkable contract that specifies file ownership, public interfaces, and dependency constraints. In the implementation stage, Developer agents generate code under a dependency-aware scheduler with bounded context and lightweight Git-based coordination, while a QA agent runs tests and drives iterative repairs. On the synthesis-based SketchEval benchmark, we explicitly compare CodeTeam's prompt-engineering (PE) and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) variants with the corresponding CodeS variants, where CodeTeam improves the overall SketchBLEU by 4.1 and 2.9 absolute points, respectively. On the execution-based NL2Repo-Bench benchmark, used as an external validation protocol, CodeTeam achieves the highest average test pass rate in both settings (34.6% PE, 42.3% SFT), confirming that the sketch-improvements extend to functional correctness under upstream test suites. Ablation results show that project-specific developer allocation and retrieval-augmented planning each contribute substantially to the SketchBLEU improvement (9.9% and 8.1% relative, respectively). CodeTeam and the experimental results are available at https://github.com/WhitenWhiten/CodeTeam
2606.22082.paper_summary_zh: 自然語言到倉庫生成（NL2Repo）需要一個系統來從自然語言需求文檔構建整個軟體倉庫。與功能級別的代碼生成相比，這項任務需要更長的規劃時間、更穩定的跨文件介面，以及對跨文件不一致性的迭代調試。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了CodeTeam，一個基於LLM的多代理框架，將規劃、決策和實施分為不同的協調階段。在規劃階段，多個架構代理草擬競爭性的軟體設計草圖（SDS），可選擇性地以檢索到的設計參考為基礎。然後，CTO代理評估、選擇並標準化最有前景的SDS，形成一個可機器檢查的合約，該合約指定文件所有權、公共介面和依賴約束。在實施階段，開發者代理在一個依賴感知的調度器下生成代碼，該調度器具有有限的上下文和輕量級的基於Git的協調，而QA代理則運行測試並推動迭代修復。在基於合成的SketchEval基準上，我們明確比較了CodeTeam的提示工程（PE）和監督微調（SFT）變體與相應的CodeS變體，其中CodeTeam的整體SketchBLEU分別提高了4.1和2.9個絕對點。在基於執行的NL2Repo-Bench基準上，作為外部驗證協議，CodeTeam在兩種設置中均達到了最高的平均測試通過率（34.6% PE，42.3% SFT），確認草圖改進延伸至上游測試套件下的功能正確性。消融結果顯示，專案特定的開發者分配和增強檢索的規劃各自對SketchBLEU的改善貢獻顯著（相對分別為9.9%和8.1%）。CodeTeam及其實驗結果可在https://github.com/WhitenWhiten/CodeTeam獲得。
2606.22082.author: Yifei Wang et.al.
2606.22082.authors: Yifei Wang, Ruiyin Li, Peng Liang, Qiong Feng, Zengyang Li, Mojtaba Shahin, Arif Ali Khan
